summary:
Polysulfide has poor dimensional stability followed closely by other types like condensation silicones which are less stable than polyethers or addition silicons known for their high dimensional consistency during setting processes. Thus, A represents an accurate ranking according to provided data sources focusing strictly on this critical property across different rubber-based impressions used clinically today. ```plaintext
The dimensional stability decreases sequentially starting from polystyrene at its worst through various forms of silicone up until polyether demonstrates optimal retention; thus,
the options should rank as: Polysulfide < Other Silicones < Polyether.
```
